answer:
**The work *San Hyan Kamahayanikan* (unknown author), Chapter 1, teaches this yogic ritual catalogue of breath maṇḍalas.**[^1]

This occurs in a section on the Seven Samādhis, after instructions on breath control (*prāṇāyāma*) and recollection of the Buddha (*buddhānusmāraṇa*). The breath in the right nostril (noted simply as "the breath in the right," implying the right nostril in this context of nāḍī practices) is visualized as:

> Now, if you desire to engage in ritual action, the breath in the right becomes a fire-maṇḍala, triangular in shape, blazing and red in color. In its center is a trident. This is your means of accomplishment for all actions.[^2]

Alternatively:

> Alternatively, make the breath in the right a Mahendra-maṇḍala, square-shaped, blazing and yellow like gold in color. In its center is an open five-pronged *vajra*. This is your means of accomplishment for the increase of life and the increase of wealth.[^3]

The breath in the left nostril becomes:

> The breath in the left becomes a formless wind-maṇḍala, black, green, or yellow in color. It blazes at both ends. Its symbol is a banner or flag. The left hand grasps a hook and a ring. This is your means of accomplishment for attraction (*ākarṣaṇa*), immobilization (*sthāmbana*), and expulsion (*uccāraṇa*).

Alternatively, as the water-maṇḍala:

> Alternatively, make the breath in the left a water-maṇḍala (*bāruṇamaṇḍala*), circular, blazing, and white in color. In its center is a subtle, small maṇḍala, like pure crystal in the middle is its form and its symbol. This is your means of accomplishment for pacification (*śāntika*).

[^1]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, Ch. 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/).

[^2]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, Ch. 1, [p-60d83c](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-60d83c).

[^3]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, Ch. 1, [p-063ac8](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-063ac8).

[^4]: *San Hyan Kamahayanikan*, Ch. 1, [p-80a477](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-80a477).
